title: Is this the proper set-up or workflow?
excerpt: I am looking for some guidance in setting up a project that I am working on. I am fairly new to Monday.com and I would like to make sure I am setting up the workflow correctly the firs time and not have to change it late…

posts.author: Taylor1430 (Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am looking for some guidance in setting up a project that I am working on. I am fairly new to Monday.com and I would like to make sure I am setting up the workflow correctly the firs time and not have to change it later.

Here is my scenario. We are a non-profit that has a program that provides services to 24 jurisdictions in our state. One of things we do is check-in with each jurisdiction to see what needs they may have. This may be monthly to every few months.

The need is to document these phone calls to make sure we are contacting them in a timely manner and also to add notes to that discussion. These notes would be short with a brief few words (Has no current needs, etc). These calls should be easily accessible for other team members to see what may be needed or who needs to be contacted.

There will also be automations that send a reminder to contact a particular jurisdiction as needed. Again, one jurisdiction may be monthly while another would be every few months.

Separately, I will likely add a means to update another board that lists each jurisdictions needs. That will come at some point.

Now, my questions. First, is the set-up. Currently; I have set up each jurisdiction as a group. Each group then has a set of columns that contain info about that jurisdiction, such as County Name, Address, Phone Number, and Point of Contact. This is in a row with each being column.

So, is it best practice to continue with this set-up and each jurisdiction as its own group? Will rows like-up as time progresses and numerous follow-ups are documented? Or, should each jurisdiction be its own board? Right now, I have minimal information so changing it would be simple.

Any thoughts on other automations I should be thinking of? At a minimum would be a notification to the Program Director a reminder to check in with that jurisdiction.

I’m trying to determine the best way to set this up yet be flexible for changes in the future. Any thoughts or feedback is greatly appreciated.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Since you’re fairly new to monday.com, I want to help set you up for long-term success with a flexible structure you won’t have to redo later.

Rather than treating each jurisdiction as its own group, I’d recommend thinking of each one as an Account. You can list all jurisdictions in a single Accounts board, where each row is a jurisdiction and columns include details like County Name, Phone, POC, etc.

From there, you can do the following:

  • Add a Connected Board for Contacts,** linking each jurisdiction to its primary point(s) of contact.**
  • Use a separate Project board to track any initiatives or services provided. Each line could represent a project or task related to a jurisdiction, or, if the work is complex, you could spin up a full board per project.
  • Track check-in activity by using the Emails & Activities widget (available on CRM plans). This gives you a timeline of past calls, notes, and emails—perfect for collaboration and visibility.
  • Set up automations on the Accounts board with a “Next Check-In Date” column. When a new note or activity is added, you can automatically push that date forward (monthly, quarterly, etc.) and notify the Program Director or assigned team member.
  • If you ever expand into tracking specific jurisdiction needs, a connected “Needs” board could link to Accounts, giving you a dedicated space for managing and updating those separately without cluttering your core data.

This structure keeps things scalable, connected, and easy to update as your team grows or your program evolves.

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Avo_FluidApps (Avo)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Taylor1430 - on this point:

Taylor:

Any thoughts on other automations I should be thinking of? At a minimum would be a notification to the Program Director a reminder to check in with that jurisdiction.

I recommend adding two columns: a Date column to log your last follow-up, and a Duration column to track how many days have passed since that date.

Then use this automation to keep the duration updated:

Every time period when the start date is set,
calculate the duration up to today.

This will automatically show how long it’s been since the last check-in.

Next, you can add this automation:

When duration is equal to X days,
notify assigned person with item name and current duration.

This setup will automatically remind the Program Director (or any assigned person) when it’s time to check in whether monthly, every few months, or on a custom schedule per jurisdiction.

It’s a simple but powerful way to stay on top of outreach and ensure timely follow-ups without manual tracking.

By the way, this is all possible using our app Smart Timeline + Duration. There are over 20 automation recipes you can explore to support flexible workflows like this.

Let me know if you need support setting this up!

posts.author: Taylor1430 (Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Desiree, Thank You so much for your response. I will most likely be reaching out to you regarding at least a portion of this. I am still getting a lot of things set up in Monday, on top of regular daily duties, so it may be a little bit. Thank You again for your response!

posts.author: Taylor1430 (Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Avo, Thank you as well! That looks like it will work well for us. I will give it a go. Thank You!!!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Sounds good! I’m here if you need anything further!
